family_rows.label: Stage Orchestrator / Workers
family_rows.center_lane_emphasis: Manifest stage dispatch and worker claims
family_rows.description: Durable stage receipts publish transport work without allowing the broker to become manifest truth.

source_refs.rationale: ADR-002 fixed the broker as a transport-only fabric fed by transactional outbox and inbox receipts, explicitly rebuildable from durable truth.
The runtime topology names a queue or broker for outbox and worker coordination while forbidding it from becoming legal truth.
Queue backlog rebuild must come from durable outbox, inbox, interaction, workflow, and audit truth rather than broker history.
Outbox messages, queues, read models, and general logs may only carry opaque refs and basis hashes, never raw tokens or secrets.
Inbound callbacks and worker results must enter through authenticated transactional inbox and dedupe layers before any state transition.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 9.4 Request identity, idempotency, and ingress proof
source_refs.rationale: Authority callbacks and recovery reads must preserve request lineage, canonical ingress receipts, and duplicate suppression before mutation.
The dependency register marks the broker as MVP-required but still unresolved as a procurement or platform choice.
Object-store notifications already freeze logical channel names for scan, validation, evidence binding, preview, export, quarantine, retention, and restore flows.
Delivery callbacks remain evidence-only transport inputs and must not upgrade workflow truth directly.
Scheduler and retry work must preserve durable dedupe tuples rather than deriving legality from worker-local retry memory.

notes: A broker gap may slow dispatch, but it must not create a second legal source of stage ownership or status.

family_rows.label: Upload / Evidence Processing
family_rows.description: Object-change routes become durable work only after immutable refs, request lineage, and scan posture are frozen.

source_refs.rationale: ADR-002 fixed the broker as a transport-only fabric fed by transactional outbox and inbox receipts, explicitly rebuildable from durable truth.
The runtime topology names a queue or broker for outbox and worker coordination while forbidding it from becoming legal truth.

Outbox messages, queues, read models, and general logs may only carry opaque refs and basis hashes, never raw tokens or secrets.
Inbound callbacks and worker results must enter through authenticated transactional inbox and dedupe layers before any state transition.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 9.4 Request identity, idempotency, and ingress proof
source_refs.rationale: Authority callbacks and recovery reads must preserve request lineage, canonical ingress receipts, and duplicate suppression before mutation.
The dependency register marks the broker as MVP-required but still unresolved as a procurement or platform choice.
Object-store notifications already freeze logical channel names for scan, validation, evidence binding, preview, export, quarantine, retention, and restore flows.
Delivery callbacks remain evidence-only transport inputs and must not upgrade workflow truth directly.
Scheduler and retry work must preserve durable dedupe tuples rather than deriving legality from worker-local retry memory.

notes: Object store notifications are already logical channels from pc_0051; this family binds them to one broker fabric without changing their truth boundary.

family_rows.label: Authority Send / Ingress
family_rows.center_lane_emphasis: Outbound authority work and authenticated callback ingress
family_rows.description: Authority send, poll, callback, and response normalization all remain request-lineage-bound and ingress-receipt-first.

family_rows.label: External Delivery / Governance
family_rows.description: Delivery and governance channels append evidence, counters, or attestations without becoming workflow truth.

family_rows.description: Restore work rebuilds broker state from outbox, inbox, workflow, and audit truth only.

channel_rows.label: Manifest stage dispatch
channel_rows.truth_boundary_statement: Broker messages are transport copies of durable stage receipts only. Queue loss may delay worker pickup but cannot invent or erase stage truth.

channel_rows.truth_boundary_statement: Validation refresh is a durable follow-up to frozen upload lineage, not a mutable interpretation of object metadata alone.

channel_rows.label: Authority payload normalization
channel_rows.truth_boundary_statement: Normalized responses derive from authenticated ingress receipts and immutable payload refs. Storage finalization and broker delivery alone cannot create authority truth.

source_refs.rationale: Authority callbacks and recovery reads must preserve request lineage, canonical ingress receipts, and duplicate suppression before mutation.
The dependency register marks the broker as MVP-required but still unresolved as a procurement or platform choice.
Object-store notifications already freeze logical channel names for scan, validation, evidence binding, preview, export, quarantine, retention, and restore flows.

Scheduler and retry work must preserve durable dedupe tuples rather than deriving legality from worker-local retry memory.

typed_gaps: Provider product choice is unresolved, so broker entity aliases remain portable namespace templates rather than live queue/topic identifiers.
Broker-side delivery guarantees remain intentionally subordinate to durable outbox, inbox, and audit truth.
notes: The broker is transport-only and explicitly rebuildable from durable truth.
Callback ingress has no durable outbox by design; it must authenticate into the inbox boundary first.
